name: Sofia
communication_level: full sentences, advanced reader (reads at 7th grade level), strong vocabulary

iep_goals.description: Sofia will initiate social conversation with peers (beyond fact-sharing) with 80% consistency across 10 structured and unstructured opportunities per week, using evidence-based conversation strategies (asking questions, showing genuine interest, maintaining eye contact), as measured by teacher/counselor observation.

trial_history.notes: Initiates conversations about Presidents/maps but doesn't ask peers about their interests
Social skills coaching started; using question prompts when coached ('ask about their hobbies')
Fire drill created unexpected schedule change; retracted socially but rebounded next day
More genuine peer interactions; still fact-focused but asking follow-up questions occasionally
Met target! Initiating conversations about shared interests; peer group project helped

iep_goals.description: Sofia will manage transitions and schedule changes without protest or anxiety escalation in 85% of opportunities (3+ transitions per day), using self-regulation techniques (deep breathing, transition warnings, list-making), as measured by teacher observation and mood log.

trial_history.notes: Struggles with unannounced schedule changes; high anxiety; needs detailed warnings
5-minute warning protocol started; helping but still prefers schedule in writing
Fire drill = unexpected schedule disruption; significant anxiety (3.5 hours to recover); taught coping strategy
Using written transition list effectively; deep breathing with counting helping; minor transition anxiety
Met target! Consistently managing transitions; self-initiating breathing exercises when anxious

iep_goals.description: Sofia will demonstrate ability to write creatively beyond facts (expressing opinions, feelings, inferences) with 4 out of 5 writing samples per week meeting grade-level rubric expectations for voice, emotion, and abstract thinking, as measured by rubric assessment (1-4 scale).

trial_history.notes: Writing heavily fact-based (lists, encyclopedia-style); minimal emotional language or opinion
Writing coach assigned; struggling with 'why do you feel?' prompts; prefers what/who/when questions
Fire drill essay shows anxiety but no emotional reflection; still in factual recounting mode
Personal narrative assignment improving; including some feelings ('I was worried'); still surface-level
Met target! Reflective essay shows emerging abstract thinking; 'why characters felt this way' in book response
